notes.content: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on L&S General Education requirements: For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps: Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements” Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley , and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). Click on “View Agreements”. Under “View Agreement by” select “ General Education/Breadth ”. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement” Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. Review all available majors at UC Berkeley .
notes.contentHtml:
COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS 


THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S)


IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond


Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or

Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended

prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on

the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. 


By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 


1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST):

·   Two transferable courses in English composition;

·   One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning;

·   Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas:

o arts and humanities

o social and behavioral sciences

o physical and biological sciences


AND


2) Major Requirements: 

Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. 


For more information on L&S General Education requirements:


For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and

Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps:

  1. Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements”
  2. Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley, and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). 
  3. Click on “View Agreements”.
  4. Under “View Agreement by” select “General Education/Breadth”. 
  5. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement”


Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. 


Review all available majors at UC Berkeley.

notes.content: Within the Theater and Performance Studie s curriculum, we engage with performance on physical, intellectual, and aesthetic levels. It is our hope that students will not only grow as performers, but also as artists, thinkers, makers, and viewers of the whole artistic event. The practical focus of the major is to create theater of all kinds using all its art forms. Whether new to theater or seasoned with years of training and skill, all students receive an education that engages them fully. Your focus may be on acting, technical theater or design, performance studies, or some combination of these. Equally important to us is the study of theater and dance performance theory, history, culture, and literature, thereby balancing the practical with the theoretical. Students engage in other upper division electives in stage management, playwriting, directing, choreography, sound design, scenic design, costume design, or lighting design, as well as a myriad of performance opportunities. The theater training and academic courses offered by our department are geared toward the student who is interested in learning about theater and performance from multiple perspectives. As either a major or minor in Theater and Performance Studies, you will study with nationally and internationally known faculty, respected for their scholarly research and for their creative work. Our graduates have gone on to professional careers in all theatrical fields and graduate schools all over the world. Our alumni include professional actors on stage and screen, arts journalists, stunt actors, directors, producers, playwrights, and more. Further departmental opportunities include student-directed theater performances and projects, honors projects, internships in local Bay Area theaters, teaching courses in the department, and invitations to diverse and exciting lectures and performances. Students also have access to world-renowned visiting guest artists through our close relationship with Cal Performances and our extensive network in the field. For more information: Laxmi Kumaran Undergraduate Academic Advisor Department of Theater, Dance and Performance Studies 15-B Dwinelle Hall (Level C) laxmik@berkeley.edu tdps.berkeley.edu
notes.contentHtml:
Within the Theater and Performance Studies curriculum, we engage with performance on physical, intellectual, and aesthetic levels. It is our hope that students will not only grow as performers, but also as artists, thinkers, makers, and viewers of the whole artistic event.


The practical focus of the major is to create theater of all kinds using all its art forms. Whether new to theater or seasoned with years of training and skill, all students receive an education that engages them fully. Your focus may be on acting, technical theater or design, performance studies, or some combination of these. Equally important to us is the study of theater and dance performance theory, history, culture, and literature, thereby balancing the practical with the theoretical. Students engage in other upper division electives in stage management, playwriting, directing, choreography, sound design, scenic design, costume design, or lighting design, as well as a myriad of performance opportunities.


The theater training and academic courses offered by our department are geared toward the student who is interested in learning about theater and performance from multiple perspectives. As either a major or minor in Theater and Performance Studies, you will study with nationally and internationally known faculty, respected for their scholarly research and for their creative work. Our graduates have gone on to professional careers in all theatrical fields and graduate schools all over the world. Our alumni include professional actors on stage and screen, arts journalists, stunt actors, directors, producers, playwrights, and more.


Further departmental opportunities include student-directed theater performances and projects, honors projects, internships in local Bay Area theaters, teaching courses in the department, and invitations to diverse and exciting lectures and performances. Students also have access to world-renowned visiting guest artists through our close relationship with Cal Performances and our extensive network in the field.


For more information:

Laxmi Kumaran

Undergraduate Academic Advisor

Department of Theater, Dance and Performance Studies

15-B Dwinelle Hall (Level C)

laxmik@berkeley.edu

tdps.berkeley.edu

notes.content: PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL No audition is required for admission into the College or the major. Auditions are required for enrollment into acting courses. Students who have not received course credit for an acting class at a previous college must start with auditioning for Fundamentals of Acting I. Students who have received course credit for one acting course may audition for Fundamentals of Acting II. Students who have received course credit fro more than one acting course may audition for Intermediate Acting. The following lower division Theater and Performance Studies courses are required for the major: 10 Fundamentals of Acting I 60 Introduction to Technical Theater and Production 26 Introduction to Performance Studies 52AC Dance in American Cultures or 25AC The Drama of American Cultures NOTES: A transferable beginning acting course comparable to THEATER 10 may be used to satisfy a major requirement. Courses comparable to THEATER 25AC, 52AC, and 26 are generally not available at the community college and must be taken at Berkeley. THEATER 25AC/52AC may not be fulfilled by any other American Cultures (AC) course. AP and IB courses do not fulfill major prerequisites.
notes.contentHtml:
PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL


No audition is required for admission into the College or the major.


Auditions are required for enrollment into acting courses. Students who have not received course credit for an acting class at a previous college must start with auditioning for Fundamentals of Acting I. Students who have received course credit for one acting course may audition for Fundamentals of Acting II. Students who have received course credit fro more than one acting course may audition for Intermediate Acting.


The following lower division Theater and Performance Studies courses are required for the major:


  • 10 Fundamentals of Acting I
  • 60 Introduction to Technical Theater and Production
  • 26 Introduction to Performance Studies
  • 52AC Dance in American Cultures or 25AC The Drama of American Cultures


NOTES:

  • A transferable beginning acting course comparable to THEATER 10 may be used to satisfy a major requirement.
  • Courses comparable to THEATER 25AC, 52AC, and 26 are generally not available at the community college and must be taken at Berkeley.
  • THEATER 25AC/52AC may not be fulfilled by any other American Cultures (AC) course.
  • AP and IB courses do not fulfill major prerequisites.
